The **2018 NRL season** was the 111th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in Australia and the 21st season run by the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"). The main competition, known as the **2018 Telstra Premiership** due to sponsorship from Telstra, featured 16 teams playing 25 weekly rounds of matches from March until September, resulting in the top eight teams playing a series of finals matches to determine which two teams reached the [2018 NRL Grand Final](/wiki/2018_NRL_Grand_Final "2018 NRL Grand Final").

## Pre-season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_NRL_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-season")]

The 2018 pre-season as usual saw a number of trials. In addition it saw [British clubs](/wiki/British_rugby_league_system "British rugby league system") [Leeds Rhinos](/wiki/Leeds_Rhinos "Leeds Rhinos"), [Hull F.C.](/wiki/Hull_F.C. "Hull F.C."), and [Wigan Warriors](/wiki/Wigan_Warriors "Wigan Warriors") conduct pre-season tours to Australia. Leeds's tour was ahead of their the [2018 World Club Challenge](/wiki/2018_World_Club_Challenge "2018 World Club Challenge") clash [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm") (won by Melbourne 38–4), while Hull's and Wigan's was ahead of them hosting a [Super League](/wiki/2018_Super_League_season "2018 Super League season") game at [Wollongong Showground](/wiki/Wollongong_Showground "Wollongong Showground").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Regular season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_NRL_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Regular season")]

With the [City vs. Country](/wiki/City_vs_Country_Origin "City vs Country Origin") representative game having been scrapped, the NRL scheduled 6 games to take place in regional areas of New South Wales and Queensland as a replacement: [Tamworth](/wiki/Tamworth,_New_South_Wales "Tamworth, New South Wales"), [Bathurst](/wiki/Bathurst,_New_South_Wales "Bathurst, New South Wales") & [Mudgee](/wiki/Mudgee "Mudgee"), and [Toowoomba](/wiki/Toowoomba "Toowoomba"), [Gladstone](/wiki/Gladstone,_Queensland "Gladstone, Queensland") & [Cairns](/wiki/Cairns "Cairns").

The overall structure of the season's draw was also revised. The second game of the [2018 State of Origin series](/wiki/2018_State_of_Origin_series "2018 State of Origin series") was played as a stand-alone fixture on a Sunday to avoid conflict with the regular season. Due to this the competition was shortened to 25 rounds and each team got 1 bye as opposed to 2 in previous years.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

## Player statistics and records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2018_NRL_season&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Player statistics and records")]

* [Jamayne Isaako](/wiki/Jamayne_Isaako "Jamayne Isaako") broke the record for the most points scored in a rookie season with 239, which was set by [Mick Cronin](/wiki/Mick_Cronin_(rugby_league) "Mick Cronin (rugby league)") in 1977 who scored 225 points.
* [Gavin Cooper](/wiki/Gavin_Cooper "Gavin Cooper") became the first forward in the 111 year history of the NRL to score a try in nine consecutive games, beating the previous record of eight set by [Frank Burge](/wiki/Frank_Burge "Frank Burge") in 1918.
* [Cameron Smith](/wiki/Cameron_Smith_(rugby_league,_born_1983) "Cameron Smith (rugby league, born 1983)") became the first hooker in NRL history to score 200 points in a season.
